title: Spain down Argentina to win World Cup and shatter Messi dream
subhead: Spain defeated Argentina 1-0, with striker Ferran Torres scoring an extra-time winning goal in the 106th minute – thus putting an end to Lionel Messi's dreams of a triumphant farewell to football's greatest stage, at what was almost certainly his last World Cup game.

'I reread my texts out of fear that they will be shared'
subhead: Le Monde's Guillemette Faure looks at the spread of screenshots of private conversations, a tactic Donald Trump has repeatedly abused.

title: An organic farmer's dismay at the speed of climate change: 'I thought I would have time to prepare'
subhead: Late frosts, cold springs and repeated heatwaves: Climate hazards now accumulate within a single season, disrupting crops and undermining the livelihoods of vegetable growers who already earn little. Even farms practicing organic agriculture, which are committed to adaptation, are struggling to cope.

title: The cinematic and audiovisual influence of the 'Odyssey,' from Méliès to Godard
subhead: Homer's ancient Greek epic poem has fueled the imaginations of countless filmmakers, giving rise to a multitude of adaptations, from sword-and-sandal epics to parodies, adult films and even Italian Westerns.

title: 20 men, a 'sperm party,' and two days of sexual torment: The brutality of the 'libertine' rapes case
subhead: Over the course of a long weekend, one of Christophe B.'s former partners was forced to endure sexual assaults by numerous men at their home. Some of these assaults were broadcast live on the swinging website Wyylde.
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

title: Christophe B., an ordinary man accused of extraordinary violence in 'libertine' rapes
subhead: The 56-year-old is accused of forcing his partners to engage in extreme sexual acts with men he recruited online for over 20 years. He is charged with rapes with torture or acts of barbarity.
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

title: 19 men charged in mass rapes case among 'libertines' in France
subhead: A man identified as Christophe B. is accused of having forced six former partners into extremely violent sexual encounters with dozens of men recruited online over a period of more than 20 years, under the guise of 'libertinism.'
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher
